ThingDef.label: unstable shortwave receiver
ThingDef.description: The exact location of the giant cluster is determined by intercepting specific short-wave signals emitted by the mechanoids. \n\nEach intercepted signal will cause the Mechanoid Hive to be alerted, and the receiver will need to perform a period of standby cover to avoid having the coordinates discovered.

ThingDef.label: giant charge turret
ThingDef.description: A steel behemoth, formed by complex craftsmanship, will only appear as a guard force in a giant cluster. \n\nThis turret will fire charge bullets with a caliber close to that of a cannonball, tearing apart each offender's body. It will switch to a shotgun-like firing mode when it is approached by the enemy.

ThingDef.label: giant charge blaster
ThingDef.description: A special charge blaster that fires large caliber charge shells.

ThingDef.label: giant inferno turret
ThingDef.description: A steel behemoth formed by complex craftsmanship, it will only appear as a guard force in giant clusters. \n\nThis turret will fire huge inferno shells, and the liquid hellfire in it will splash in all directions after the shells explode, melting all the enemies around.

ThingDef.label: giant inferno launcher
ThingDef.description: A special gun that fires large caliber inferno.

ThingDef.label: charge enhancer
ThingDef.description: A structurally sophisticated amplifier that can significantly increase the firing speed and accuracy of the surrounding giant charge blaster.

ThingDef.label: plasma enhancer
ThingDef.description: A sophisticatedly structured enhancer that can significantly increase the firing speed of the surrounding giant inferno cannons.

ThingDef.label: archotech trick mine
ThingDef.description: A small device that hovers eerily in mid-air and can only be vaguely observed when extremely close to it. The \n\n triggers will vaporize everything around it.

DamageDef.deathMessage: {0} has been beaten into quantum foam.

ThingDef.label: giant auto mortar
ThingDef.description: A steel beast assembled by a complex process would only appear as a guard force in a giant cluster. \n\nA single cluster antigrain shell is powerful enough to cut a mountain down by several meters. It will switch to landing fire mode when it is approached by the enemy.

ThingDef.label: giant auto mortar
ThingDef.description: Mortars that fire cluster anti-grain shells.

ThingDef.label: None
ThingDef.description: None.

ThingDef.label: mechanical container
ThingDef.description: Containers containing large amounts of basic supplies for building clusters normally require a password to unlock them, but we obviously don't have to follow this rule.

ThingDef.label: psychic shielding marrow
ThingDef.description: A huge archotech building capable of shielding the psychic network of all enemies in the region. \n\n Destroy to unlock Level 1 Cluster Technology.

ThingDef.label: psychic suppression marrow
ThingDef.description: A huge archotech building capable of suppressing the consciousness of all enemies in the region to a lower level. \n\n Destroy to unlock Level 2 Cluster Technology.

ThingDef.label: psychic berserk marrow
ThingDef.description: A huge archotech building capable of intermittently raging against any enemy in the region. \n\n Destroy to unlock Level 3 Cluster technology.

ThingDef.label: giant auto mortar (defensive)
ThingDef.description: A steel beast assembled by a complex process would only appear as a guard force in a giant cluster. \n\nYou can order the intelligent colonists in your party to hack into the mortar and use its powerful fire to damage the giant exostrider.

ThingDef.label: giant auto mortar
ThingDef.description: Mortars that fire cluster anti-grain shells.

ThingDef.label: exostrider
ThingDef.description:
A giant central mechanoid, one of the most magnificent creations of the mechaonid hive. 

Normally, the exostrider would only use its transponder to uniformly schedule the mechanoid clusters within a galaxy and exchange information directly with the mechanoid hive core. A exostrider usually has several mechanoid ships working together to facilitate information transmission and protection. In special situations, it can also use heavy artillery and industrial rapid-fire artillery combat, rapid-fire guns will automatically search for a certain range of enemy attacks, while the main gun will only be activated when forced to use against some of the most threatening targets. 

Since the exostrider acts as a "coordinate origin" when summoning the mechanoid fleet, it can't move at will, but its heavy armor also means you can't deal damage to it using normal methods.

ThingDef.label: "Gate of Hell" super heavy artillery
ThingDef.description: A giant cannon mounted by a giant exostrider, whose single fire was enough to shake the whole earth. It's usually only activated when there's a major threat.

ThingDef.label: "Styx Starlight" rapid-fire cannon
ThingDef.description: A rapid-fire cannon equipped with a exostrider, usually used to destroy groups of heavily armored units, but had limited effectiveness against small, flexible targets.

ThingDef.label: charge blaster turret
ThingDef.description: A small charge blaster designed for use on a defense turret.
